title: Review of radiation-hardening benchmarks for CubeSat power systems
abstract: We review radiation-hardening benchmarks for CubeSat power systems and ask whether comparator choice, uncertainty reporting, and failure-condition disclosure are consistent enough for cross-study synthesis. Figure 1 maps the thematic evidence structure, Table 1 summarizes the benchmark families and scope limits, and the manuscript states the failure condition that breaks cross-mission comparability.
manuscript_text: This review synthesizes benchmark families for CubeSat power-system radiation hardening, asks whether the literature is internally comparable, and defines the synthesis workflow used to group the evidence. Figure 1 maps the thematic evidence structure, Table 1 summarizes comparator choice and scope limits, and the discussion states the uncertainty around cross-mission transfer together with the failure condition that breaks comparability when reporting boundaries are absent. Appendix A lists the grouping rules and synthesis workflow for the reviewed studies.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Thematic evidence structure across CubeSat radiation-hardening benchmark studies.
tables: Table 1. Comparator choice, uncertainty reporting, failure conditions, and scope limits across the reviewed studies.
references: Ref1 CubeSat power-system radiation benchmark study.
Ref4 uncertainty reporting in radiation-hardening studies.
Ref5 failure-condition disclosure in spacecraft electronics work.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A lists the reviewed studies, grouping rules, and synthesis workflow.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
